title: Korean Symbols: Copy & Paste 사랑 Hangul Characters
meta_description: Korean symbols and Hangul to copy and paste — words like 사랑 (love), K-pop fan slang, jamo letters, and ㅋㅋㅋ text laughs. Click any symbol to copy it instantly.
hero_tagline: Hangul words, jamo letters, and K-pop fan slang — each labeled with romanization and meaning. Click any symbol to copy it instantly.
intro: Korean symbols have gone global with K-pop and K-drama fandom, and Hangul words like 사랑 (sarang, love) now appear in bios, fan edits, and comments everywhere. This library collects meaningful Korean words, the building-block jamo letters of the Hangul alphabet, and the text slang Koreans actually use — like ㅋㅋㅋ for laughter — all labeled with romanization and meaning. Copy them for fan accounts, captions, or learning practice.

sections.label: Hangul Words
sections.intro: Beautiful Korean words for love, dreams, and nature — the most copied Hangul for bios and captions.

sections.label: K-Pop & Fan Culture
sections.intro: The fan vocabulary of K-pop comments and stan Twitter, with romanization so you use each word right.

sections.label: Jamo Letters
sections.intro: The individual consonants and vowels of the Hangul alphabet, from the Hangul Compatibility Jamo block.

sections.label: Text Slang
sections.intro: Jamo shorthand used in Korean chats — laughter, tears, and quick replies.

collection_section.label: Hangul Sets
collection_section.intro: Copy a full Hangul set — fan cheers, laugh strings, or a ready-made K-pop bio frame — in one click.
